textContent:
Good afternoon everyone, I am Miguel Alexis M. Javier and before I start.
Let me ask you this, how do you spend your free time? Do you play online games? When is the last time you played online games? If you play online games, did you know that it actually has some advantages and disadvantages. Due to the emergence of online games, more and more people started to play online games. It made quite an impact to some of us. 

Online gaming was once just a way to connect with your friends. But as time passed, players started getting obsessed which eventually led to addiction. From what started from a fun game to play just to past off time turned in to a problem that has been limitting people from functioning. According to an investigation by SEM-Neural Network Approach, adolescents and children are the most abundant players of online gaming. Online gaming can also affect the loss of interest in education, which causes more children and adolescents to fail in school. Not to mention the number of negative things that online gaming cause, like eye strain, fatigue,  and others.

Despite these things, online gaming can also provide benefits for us. Talking with other players can help you improve your communication skills and your cooperation with others. It can also improve one's leadership and strategizing which helps with real life applications. Even just making a decision in an online live battle can be a benefit for you. It tests you on how you make quick decision-making and strategize in a short amount of time. Another benefit is also that it can boost our creativity in sandbox games. Sandbox games allows us to create and build anything we want which will boost our creativity.

Online games made quite an impact on our lives, doesn't it? Based on the stated impacts, We can conclude that despite the disadvantages, online games still have its good sides. It enabled us to be smarter with our decision-making and strategy building. If managed properly, it can enable us to be smarter, and build connections. But if left unchecked, it can lead to addiction and poor health. In the end, it' sa not just about playing, it's about playing responsibly.

Good afternoon everyone, I am Miguel Alexis M. Javier and before I start.

Let me ask you this, how do you spend your free time? Do you play online games? When is the last time you played online games? If you play online games, did you know that it actually has some advantages and disadvantages. Due to the emergence of online games, more and more people started to play online games. It made quite an impact to some of us. 


Online gaming was once just a way to connect with your friends. But as time passed, players started getting obsessed which eventually led to addiction. From what started from a fun game to play just to past off time turned in to a problem that has been limitting people from functioning. According to an investigation by SEM-Neural Network Approach, adolescents and children are the most abundant players of online gaming. Online gaming can also affect the loss of interest in education, which causes more children and adolescents to fail in school. Not to mention the number of negative things that online gaming cause, like eye strain, fatigue,  and others.


Despite these things, online gaming can also provide benefits for us. Talking with other players can help you improve your communication skills and your cooperation with others. It can also improve one's leadership and strategizing which helps with real life applications. Even just making a decision in an online live battle can be a benefit for you. It tests you on how you make quick decision-making and strategize in a short amount of time. Another benefit is also that it can boost our creativity in sandbox games. Sandbox games allows us to create and build anything we want which will boost our creativity.


Online games made quite an impact on our lives, doesn't it? Based on the stated impacts, We can conclude that despite the disadvantages, online games still have its good sides. It enabled us to be smarter with our decision-making and strategy building. If managed properly, it can enable us to be smarter, and build connections. But if left unchecked, it can lead to addiction and poor health. In the end, it' sa not just about playing, it's about playing responsibly.
